question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger connection pool exhaustion by flooding the Rosetta API with concurrent requests, causing ConnectToDb() to silently log a warning via log.Warn(err) instead of failing hard, so that subsequent mirror node record queries return incomplete or missing data?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ConnectToDb() calls log.Warn(err) on a failed gorm.Open() but continues execution, causing db.DB() to be called on a nil *gorm.DB, returning nil from ConnectToDb() and silently dropping all record exports to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user cause ConnectToDb() to return nil (when sqlDb, err := db.DB() fails) without any panic or hard stop, so that callers of ConnectToDb() receive a nil DbClient and silently skip exporting records to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user send a high volume of API requests that exhaust sqlDb.SetMaxOpenConns(dbConfig.Pool.MaxOpenConnections), causing new DB queries to queue indefinitely and mirror node record exports to be delayed or dropped entirely?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sslmode=disable setting in GetDsn() (used by ConnectToDb()) to perform a man-in-the-middle attack on the unencrypted PostgreSQL connection, injecting or suppressing records before they are exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated failed connection attempts to exhaust sqlDb.SetMaxIdleConns(dbConfig.Pool.MaxIdleConnections), causing idle connection starvation and subsequent record queries to mirror nodes to return stale or missing data?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a connection retry mechanism in ConnectToDb() so that a transient network partition causes the mirror node to permanently export zero records for the duration of the outage without any recovery?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user cause ConnectToDb() to silently succeed with a broken DB handle (gorm.Open returns error but non-nil db) by exploiting GORM's lazy connection behavior, leading to all subsequent record exports to mirror nodes failing silently?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user craft requests that pass a nil context to GetDbWithContext(), triggering the ctx == nil branch that creates a context.Background(), and then exploit the statementTimeout to cause premature query cancellation, resulting in partial or missing records exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the condition d.statementTimeout <= 0 in GetDbWithContext() to bypass the context timeout entirely (returning the noop cancel function), allowing unbounded long-running queries that block the connection pool and starve other record export operations?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where GetDbWithContext() returns a context that is already cancelled (due to a very short statementTimeout), causing all DB queries for record exports to mirror nodes to fail with context deadline exceeded and return empty results?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the #nosec G118 suppression on context.WithTimeout() in GetDbWithContext() to cause a context leak when the returned cancel function is not called by the caller, exhausting goroutine/context resources and causing missing record exports?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent requests that each trigger GetDbWithContext() with a very small statementTimeout, causing a flood of context.WithTimeout() goroutines that are never cancelled (noop path), leading to resource exhaustion and dropped record exports to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that GetDbWithContext() does not validate d.db for nil before calling d.db.WithContext(childCtx), causing a nil pointer dereference panic that crashes the mirror node export process when ConnectToDb() previously returned nil?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: GetDb()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a code path where GetDb() returns a nil *gorm.DB (because ConnectToDb() returned nil and NewDbClient was never called), causing all record export queries to mirror nodes to panic or silently fail?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: NewDbClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that NewDbClient() does not validate the db parameter for nil, so that a nil *gorm.DB is wrapped in a client struct and later used in GetDb() or GetDbWithContext(), causing silent failures in record exports to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the MaxLifetime pool setting (sqlDb.SetConnMaxLifetime) by timing requests to coincide with connection recycling, causing in-flight record export queries to be interrupted and return incomplete data to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of TLS (sslmode=disable in GetDsn()) to intercept the PostgreSQL wire protocol and replay or suppress specific record export transactions, causing mirror nodes to receive incorrect historical data?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a denial-of-service by exhausting all MaxOpenConnections slots via concurrent API requests, so that record export queries queue beyond their statement timeout and return errors, resulting in missing records at mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ConnectToDb() does not implement connection health checks or reconnection logic, so that a stale connection in the pool silently returns empty result sets for record export queries to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the time.Duration(d.statementTimeout)*time.Second conversion in GetDbWithContext() by triggering a configuration where statementTimeout is set to a very large integer, causing integer overflow in the duration calculation and producing an effectively zero or negative timeout that skips record exports?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the gormlogrus.New() logger attached in ConnectToDb() to cause log injection by crafting inputs that produce misleading log entries, obscuring genuine record export failures from mirror node operators?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated API calls that cause the connection pool to cycle through MaxLifetime expiry, creating a window where all connections are simultaneously recycled and record export queries to mirror nodes return errors or empty results?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the noop cancel function returned when statementTimeout <= 0 to cause context propagation failures, where a parent context cancellation is not properly forwarded to the DB query, allowing stale or incorrect records to be exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ConnectToDb() uses log.Errorf (not a fatal/panic) when db.DB() fails, allowing the application to continue running with a nil DbClient and silently export zero records to mirror nodes indefinitely?
